title: role allowed_reads scoped playbooks lane tasks
purpose: F6.2 defines role allowed reads scoped playbooks lane tasks for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling.
scope: Owns `F6.2` only; siblings under `F6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).

Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §8 — Branch F — Organization plane (template-packs = ceiling)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#8-branch-f-organization-plane-template-packs-ceiling).
behavior_steps: When staleness.json or a program artifact graph flags stale nodes on paths in the next implement scope, the conductor invokes reconcile-stale or reconcile-artifact-graph before spawning workers—not after partial edits land.

S0 scripts maintain stale flags file-derived; the reconcile skills decide whether to rerun the affected design phase, waive with H2 operator approval, or enqueue hierarchy deepen items for impacted ids.
Workers on affected paths pause while reconciliation runs; pursuit resumes only after flags clear or an explicit H2 waiver is dual-written to journal and state.json.

In program mode, reconcile-artifact-graph consults [C4.4](C4.4-artifact-graph-per-program-and-pack.md) cross-stream contracts alongside [E5.1](E5.1-staleness-design-graph-staleness-json.md) as the design-graph source of truth.
If implement spawn would proceed against stale design without reconciliation or waiver, pursuit halts at H2—silent implement violates Plane E contract and invalidates goal_verify evidence.

Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `F6.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

implementation: Per-role allowed_reads in lane.json work orders.
Validate `F6.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `F6.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `F6.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: F6.2 defines role allowed reads scoped playbooks lane tasks for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling. In practice, the system when staleness.json or a program artifact graph flags stale nodes on paths in the next implement scope, the conductor invokes reconcile-stale or reconcile-artifact-graph before spawning workers—not after partial edits land, then s0 scripts maintain stale flags file-derived; the reconcile skills decide whether to rerun the affected design phase, waive with h2 operator approval, or enqueue hierarchy deepen items for impacted ids.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-f 2026-06-28 -->

Per-role `allowed_reads` scopes workers to role playbooks, lane task cards, facts entries, and design slices declared in pack YAML—not the full vision monolith or unrelated department lanes. Librarian returns paths within this cap (max five) before implement spawn ([B2.2](B2.2-librarian-allowed-reads-catalog-composition.md)).

Scope bleed (QA worker reading finance playbooks) violates pack intent and risks cross-department edits. allowed_reads updates on active_role rotation ([F6.1](F6.1-role-mapping-conductor-context-switch-active-role.md)). Lane.json work orders may further narrow reads for parallel slots. Forbidden paths are explicit deny list alongside allowed_reads.
